content_pages.body_md:
# JVTO Inclusions & Exclusions Policy

Policy Route: `/policy/inclusions-exclusions/`

### 1) Purpose

This policy defines what is included and not included in JVTO private tour packages. Only inclusions written on your specific **Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)** are contractually binding.

---

### 2) Contractual Rule (Write-it-to-Bind-it)

In this policy, “Official Tour Voucher” refers to your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) issued by JVTO after successful payment.

Only inclusions that appear on:

- the official JVTO package page for your chosen tour; and/or
- your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)

are legally binding components of your package.

If it is not written in your confirmed inclusions and voucher, it is not part of the contracted package, even if discussed informally.

---

### 3) Standard Inclusions (All Eligible JVTO Packages)

Unless stated otherwise on your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF), a confirmed JVTO private tour package typically includes:

#### 3.1 Transportation

- Private air-conditioned vehicle for all land sectors listed in your confirmed itinerary
- Fuel, tolls, and standard parking fees as per itinerary
- Vehicle allocation by group size (comfort and safety):

  - 2–3 guests: 1 × MPV (e.g., Toyota Avanza or similar)
  - 4–9 guests: 1 × Toyota Hiace (or similar 16-seat minibus)
  - 10–11 guests: 1 × Toyota Hiace + 1 × MPV (for seating and luggage space)

- 4WD Jeep at Mount Bromo (when Bromo is included):

  - Private jeep(s) for sunrise and crater viewpoints
  - Max. ±4 guests per jeep for comfort and safety

#### 3.2 Accommodation

- Hotel / homestay accommodation for all overnight stays listed in your itinerary
- Standard allocation (unless otherwise stated on your voucher):

  - Even-numbered groups: 1 room per 2 guests (King or Twin)
  - Odd-numbered groups: 1 room per 2 guests + 1 extra bed in one room

- Rooming principles:

  - Maximum 2 guests per standard room (excluding the designated extra bed where applicable)
  - King beds for couples or guests preferring more space
  - Twin beds for guests sharing a room

- Upgrade option (subject to request and availability): replacing an extra bed with an additional private room may be available at a published supplement (as per voucher)

#### 3.3 Professional Crew

- Licensed, vetted crew adjusted by group size and route:

  - 2–3 guests: 1 × driver-guide (English-speaking) + licensed local site guides where required
  - 4–11 guests: 1 × professional driver + 1 × escort guide + licensed local site guides at key locations
  - Additional drivers allocated when extra vehicles are used

- Crew duties include: safe driving, time management, coordination with hotels/jeeps/ferries/park authorities, and basic route explanations and guest support

#### 3.4 Tickets, Permits & Local Logistics

All listed entrance tickets and environmental/park permits connected to your confirmed itinerary, where applicable, such as:

- Mount Bromo National Park access
- Mount Ijen (including regular entrance and applicable summit/crater access where legally permitted)
- Madakaripura Waterfall
- Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
- Other named attractions in your confirmed program
- Local retribution / standard local fees where explicitly built into the package

#### 3.5 Mount Ijen Safety & Trekking Support (When Ijen is Included)

- Professional gas mask (rated for sulfur environment) for each participating guest
- Trekking poles (shared or individual, depending on configuration)
- Mandatory Ijen medical check & digital/printed clearance arranged by JVTO, conducted by licensed medical personnel at an approved facility or at your hotel in the Bondowoso area (included in the package for itineraries that visit Ijen)

#### 3.6 Meals & Water

- Daily bottled mineral water in the vehicle for overland sectors
- Hotel breakfasts for each included overnight stay
- Any additional included meals (e.g., set dinners or lunches) are included only when explicitly stated on the official package page and/or your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)

#### 3.7 Java-Bali Ferry (Where Applicable)

Ferry tickets for Java-Bali or Bali-Java sectors when:

- the crossing is listed in your itinerary; and
- the ticket is specified as included on your voucher

#### 3.8 JVTO Branded Inclusions & Group Extras

- 1 × JVTO travel T-shirt per participant for eligible packages (standard JVTO design; size selection via “Complete Your Details”)
- Custom JVTO Group T-shirt (where explicitly included): for groups of ≥12 guests in a single booking, if written on your package/voucher; 1 × custom design T-shirt per person; sizes and final design must be confirmed before production

#### 3.9 On-Trip Support & Coordination

- Pre-trip assistance via official WhatsApp/email
- Clear pickup instructions and emergency contact numbers on your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)
- On-trip coordination for timing, weather/closure adjustments, and safety decisions
- Support in case of incident (basic first response, coordination with local clinics/hospitals, and logistics adjustments where feasible)

---

### 4) “We Offer More” — Conditional Inclusions (Only If Written)

The following are included **only** when clearly stated on both:

- the official package description you booked; and
- your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)

Conditional inclusions may include:

- Custom JVTO group T-shirts (design, quantity, eligibility conditions)
- Additional hosted meals (e.g., dinner in Bondowoso before Ijen; lunch after Tumpak Sewu; special farewell dinner)
- Bali hotel/airport transfers beyond the standard ferry crossing
- Early check-in / late check-out at hotels
- Dedicated photographer/videographer services
- Police escort or special convoy arrangements for VIP/large groups (subject to availability and regulation)

**Extra meals — specific itinerary conditions (only if written):**

- For Ijen + Bondowoso overnight itineraries:

  - Dinner at the Bondowoso hotel before the Ijen night trek
  - Lunch at the Bondowoso hotel after returning from Ijen

- For itineraries including Tumpak Sewu:

  - One additional lunch at/near Yanto Homestay (or equivalent local partner) after the Tumpak Sewu program

---

### 5) Exclusions (Simple Principle)

To keep things clear: anything that is not explicitly listed as an inclusion on the official package page and on your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) is excluded.

Common examples of not included items (unless clearly written as included for your specific booking):

- International / domestic flights and entry visas
- Travel insurance of any kind
- Meals not specified (including alcohol)
- Tips/gratuities for guides and drivers

---

### 6) Discrepancies

This policy is designed to integrate with your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF). In the event of any discrepancy, the version referenced on your booking confirmation and voucher will apply.

---

# Policy 3 — JVTO Privacy & Data Protection Policy

content_pages.meta_description: JVTO privacy and data protection: what data is collected, why it is used, when it is shared for operations, and how to request corrections.
content_pages.body_md:
# JVTO Privacy & Data Protection Policy

Policy Route: `/policy/privacy`

### 1) Purpose

This policy explains what personal data JVTO collects, why it is collected, how it is used, and when it may be shared to operate your tour booking and comply with applicable requirements.

---

### 2) What Data We Collect

JVTO may collect the following categories of data, depending on your booking and itinerary requirements:

**2.1 Contact and booking details (lead guest):**

- Full name
- Email address
- WhatsApp/mobile number
- Booking references and tour details needed to deliver services

**2.2 Participant details (where required):**

- Guest list for accommodation and permits
- Passport/ID details and date of birth where required for permits, tickets, or legal compliance
- Pickup/drop-off details, rooming preferences, and operational notes required to deliver services

**2.3 Safety and logistics information (where relevant):**

- Dietary needs and relevant health information provided by you for safety planning and operational coordination (only where necessary)

---

### 3) Why We Collect and Use Your Data

JVTO uses personal data to:

- Issue and deliver your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) and manage your booking
- Coordinate logistics before and during your tour (pickup timing, accommodation, vehicles, guides, route planning)
- Provide customer support and operational assistance
- Meet legal, permit, ticketing, and accommodation registration requirements where applicable
- Support safety processes and emergency response coordination where needed

---

### 4) When We Share Your Data

JVTO shares personal data only when required to deliver the booked services or comply with applicable requirements. This may include sharing necessary details with:

- Accommodation partners (for check-in/registration and rooming)
- Drivers, escort guides, local site guides, and operational teams (for coordination and guest support)
- Ticketing/permit partners and local authorities where permits or entry requirements apply
- Medical screening providers or approved facilities where mandatory screening applies (e.g., where required by authorities for specific sites)

JVTO does not share data beyond what is necessary for the specific operational purpose.

---

### 5) Payment Data

- Card payments are processed through a secure checkout and authorised payment gateway.
- JVTO does not store full card numbers, CVV codes, or online banking passwords.

---

### 6) Data Protection and Access

JVTO applies internal controls to protect personal data and limits access to authorised personnel and partners only when needed for service delivery.

---

### 7) Contact

For privacy or data-related requests, contact:

- Email: `hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com`
- WhatsApp: `+62 822-4478-8833`

intro_paragraphs: Use this Travel Guide as the official handbook for planning private tours to Mount Bromo, Kawah Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu with Java Volcano Tour Operator (JVTO).

sections.title: Hero Intro
paragraphs: Use this Travel Guide as JVTO’s official handbook for planning private tours to Mount Bromo, Kawah Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu. Java Volcano Tour Operator (JVTO) is presented here as a licensed Indonesian inbound operator based in Bondowoso, specialising in private, all-inclusive routes to East Java’s key volcano and waterfall destinations.

Understand how JVTO handles safety, health, weather, closures, payments, and student deals before you book or travel. This page summarises the key topics and directs you to dedicated detail pages for each subject.

Use your Official E-Voucher / Invoice and the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy as the final reference for legal or case-specific details. This Travel Guide is a plain-language summary designed to help guests understand how things work, but binding terms remain in your official booking documents.
Primary CTAs
How to book & pay → /travel-guide/booking-information
Browse private tours → /tours

sections.title: Latest JVTO Update (Placeholder Block)
paragraphs: Check this “Latest JVTO update” card for time-sensitive operational notes before departure. This section is reserved for information your team can confirm and update routinely.

Only publish what you can reasonably confirm at the time of update. Do not include rumours, assumptions, or unverified claims.

sections.title: JVTO Operating Status (East Java Volcano Routes)
paragraphs: Treat JVTO’s operating status as an operations update for JVTO routes—not a country-level travel advisory. JVTO currently operates private, pre-booked tours on standard East Java routes, subject to weather, volcanic activity, and access regulations.
Expect tour plans to follow official sources and local access rules. We plan and adjust tours based on official information (including MAGMA / PVMBG) and local park management.
Expect partial restrictions even when tours are running. Some viewpoints, hiking sections, or waterfall access may be temporarily restricted even when the tour program continues.
Expect itinerary changes to follow the rules in Weather & Closures and Booking, Payment & Cancellation. Changes are handled according to those pages and your booking documents.
Check your own government’s travel advice for country-level alerts. Guests should consult official travel advisories issued by their own governments in addition to this guide.

sections.title: On This Page (In-page Navigation)
paragraphs: Use this section as a quick index of the key topics.

sections.title: Safety on JVTO Tours (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect JVTO tours to be run as private-only, pre-briefed programs with defined safety procedures. JVTO is presented here as a licensed inbound operator led by an active Tourist Police officer in East Java, and all tours are stated to be private-only (no mixed groups, no transport-only tickets).

Expect routing and decisions to adapt to conditions and regulations. We monitor official information on volcanic activity and weather, apply safety procedures on roads and trails, and adjust itineraries when conditions or regulations make the original plan unsafe.

sections.title: Health & Ijen Screening (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect Ijen night hikes to require screening and to remain subject to safety decisions even after clearance. Kawah Ijen is described here as a night hike at altitude with possible sulfur gas exposure.

Expect screening to be included for eligible JVTO guests and to be digitally verifiable. For JVTO guests joining Ijen hikes, the package includes a formal health screening by trained medical staff, recorded digitally and verifiable via QR code.

Expect the public tool to exist, and expect hikes to be modified or cancelled if conditions are unsafe. The same digital system is described as available to other travellers via a public tool. A cleared screening reduces risk but does not remove it; hikes may still be cancelled or modified based on conditions.

sections.title: Weather, Volcano Alerts & Closures (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect natural conditions to change access and require adjustments to plans. Heavy rain, landslides, volcanic ash, gas, smoke, floods, forest fires, road damage, or official ceremonies can affect access to Bromo, Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu.
Expect JVTO to distinguish partial closures from full closures and propose alternatives where possible. Some issues affect only one viewpoint or path; others trigger full closure.

Expect Travel Credit options to follow the Booking Policy and your E-Voucher. When changes are required, JVTO applies the options defined in the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and your Official E-Voucher / Invoice, including Travel Credit where applicable.

sections.title: Packing & Fitness (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect these routes to be non-technical but physically demanding for many travellers. Early starts, night hiking, cold winds, steep paths, stairs, and wet or slippery sections can apply across Bromo, Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu.

Expect the guide to provide self-checks and packing lists, not medical approval. The Packing & Fitness guide explains a basic self-check, what to pack for all routes, and extra items for Bromo sunrise, Ijen night hikes, and Tumpak Sewu.
Expect final hike decisions to follow screening, on-site conditions, and safety assessments. The guide is a preparation reference; it does not override screening or safety decisions.

sections.title: Booking, Payments & Travel Credit (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect JVTO bookings to operate as private, all-inclusive tours on a pre-booked basis. The Booking Information page explains booking requests, confirmation steps, deposits, payment timing, and methods.

Expect Travel Credit to be the core cancellation mechanism rather than cash refunds. The summary references Travel Credit, how it is used, and why cash refunds are not provided, balanced by Travel Credit flexibility.
Expect different time windows to affect cancellations and changes. The page describes changes, reschedules, and cancellations across different timelines.

sections.title: Traffic Police Escort for Groups (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect escort to be considered only for larger groups and only through formal approval. For larger groups (example: around 18 guests or more with multi-vehicle convoys), JVTO may propose requesting official traffic police escort for specific road segments.
Expect escort to be official, conditional, documented, and not automatic. Escort is provided by the official traffic police unit, subject to law, written approval, and availability.
Expect escort to be for safe convoy movement—not to bypass rules or guarantee faster travel. If approved, details and any charges are listed in your quotation and Official E-Voucher / Invoice.
Expect no escort unless it is explicitly written. If escort is not stated in your booking documents, the tour should be considered to run without escort.

sections.title: ISIC Student Deals (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Expect ISIC student deals to be presented as a fairness mechanism against higher foreign fees. Through collaboration with ISIC, JVTO describes student-focused structures on selected tours for verified students.

Expect eligibility and verification details to be defined on the ISIC page. The ISIC page explains eligible tours, how to present valid ISIC identification, and how student pricing interacts with standard booking and Travel Credit rules.

sections.title: Official Sources & External Links (Panel Summary)
paragraphs: Use official Indonesian sources and your own government’s travel advice alongside this Travel Guide. JVTO states it monitors official Indonesian authorities for planning and adjustments.
Use MAGMA Indonesia / PVMBG for volcano and geohazard context, and VONA for advanced aviation-related notices. Links are presented as references rather than copied content.
Use local park and government announcements for temporary closures and ceremony-related restrictions. Access rules may change due to closures, restricted zones, and official events.
Use your own government’s travel advisory for country-level security, health, and entry requirements. JVTO positions this guide as complementary to—not a replacement for—government advisories.

paragraphs: Treat your Official E-Voucher / Invoice as the highest authority for your specific booking. If there is ever a difference between website wording and booking documents, the order of priority is:
Verify uncertainties via official JVTO channels before travel. Contact JVTO using the official contact details listed on the website if you are unsure how a rule applies.

travel_pages.meta_description: Answers to common questions about JVTO private tours: inclusions, payments, Travel Credit policy, Ijen health screening, closures, and student discounts.
intro_paragraphs: Plain-language answers to the questions we receive most often about JVTO private tours.
For binding rules, always refer to your Official E-Voucher and the Policy pages.
Official channels: Website https://javavolcano-touroperator.com • WhatsApp +6282244788833 • Email hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com.

sections.title: Intro
paragraphs: Use this FAQ for quick, plain-language answers to common JVTO questions. This page answers the questions we receive most often about our private Bromo, Ijen and Tumpak Sewu tours.

Use official documents for binding rules. For detailed rules, always refer to your Official E-Voucher and the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy, as well as the Booking Information and Ijen Health Screening pages in this Travel Guide.

sections.title: A) Private Tours, Inclusions & How to Book
paragraphs: Q1. Are your tours private or shared?
All JVTO routes are private tours only. We do not sell shared-group buses, “join in” departures, or transport-only deals, and each itinerary is designed and operated for your group only.
Q2. What is included in the price?
Use your tour page and Official E-Voucher as the inclusion checklist. Each tour page has a detailed What’s Included section, and your Official E-Voucher confirms what applies to your booking.

Expect essential logistics to be covered where listed. Depending on the itinerary, packages typically include park fees and permits as listed, private transport and driver (vehicle, fuel, tolls, parking), licensed local guides as relevant, safety gear where needed (for example, gas mask and trekking pole for Ijen), unlimited mineral water, JVTO travel T-shirt, all hotel breakfasts, extra meals in Bondowoso during the Ijen segment, extra lunch after Tumpak Sewu where stated, and Ijen health screening on Ijen night-hike tours.
Expect standard exclusions unless stated. Travel insurance, visas, tips, personal expenses, and optional extras not listed are usually not included.
Q3. How do I make a booking?

Confirm booking by paying through secure checkout and receiving an Official E-Voucher. Choose a tour on the official JVTO website, fill in lead guest details, pay the deposit or full amount via secure checkout, and receive your Official E-Voucher and receipt by email.
Treat payment \+ issued voucher as confirmation. Your booking is confirmed only after payment has been processed and your Official E-Voucher has been issued.

sections.title: B) Payments, Travel Credit & Changes
paragraphs: Q4. How do deposits and balance payments work?

Expect a 20% deposit in most cases and up to 100% for last-minute departures. The remaining balance is usually paid by card no later than 5 days before Day 1 or by bank transfer no later than 3 days before Day 1, as described in your Official E-Voucher and the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy.
Q5. What is JVTO Travel Credit?

Use Travel Credit instead of cash refunds when cancelling early enough. Travel Credit is issued in IDR, is equal to up to 100% of eligible payments received, has no expiry date, can be transferred or gifted with written confirmation, and has no rebooking or administration fee.
Do not expect Travel Credit for very late cancellations or no-shows. Travel Credit does not apply to very late cancellations or no-shows.
Q6. Can I change my dates or details after booking?

Expect minor corrections to be possible and supplier-impact changes to add costs. Minor corrections may be possible without extra charge, but changes affecting permits, tickets, hotel inventory, or vehicle/crew scheduling typically pass supplier costs on to the guest, and reschedule rules follow the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy.

sections.title: C) Safety, Ijen Health Screening & Closures
paragraphs: Q7. How does the Ijen health screening work?

Ijen screening is included for JVTO night-hike tours and recorded digitally. Trained medical staff perform basic checks, results are recorded in a digital system and linked to a QR code, and gate staff can verify that screening occurred.

Treat safety outcomes as itinerary decisions, not automatic refunds. If screening suggests it is unsafe to hike, alternatives are discussed in line with the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and your Official E-Voucher, and health-related decisions do not automatically mean a refund.
Q8. Do you guarantee blue fire or certain views at Ijen or Bromo?
No, blue fire and views are not guaranteed. Sightings and views depend on weather, wind, gas levels, crowding, and official regulations, and closures or restrictions may apply.

Expect JVTO to follow rules and apply policy-based options. JVTO follows authorities and applies alternative-route and Travel Credit options as described in the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy.
Q9. What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed?

Expect closures to trigger alternatives where possible and policy-based remedies. Closures can happen due to volcanic activity, toxic gas, forest fires, floods, landslides, ceremonies, or regulations, and JVTO will inform you, suggest alternatives where available, and apply rules in the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and your Official E-Voucher.

sections.title: D) Groups, Students & Extras
paragraphs: Q10. Can you arrange a traffic police escort for our group?

Escort can be coordinated for larger groups only when approved by authorities. JVTO can help request official traffic police escort for certain road segments for larger groups, but escorts are not automatic, depend on approvals and availability, and any costs are confirmed in writing.
Q11. Do you offer ISIC student deals?
Yes, ISIC deals apply to selected packages for verified card holders. The exact benefit and eligible tours are described on the ISIC page.
Q12. Can you handle large groups such as schools or incentive trips?
Yes, JVTO can design private multi-vehicle programs for larger groups. Programs may include convoy planning, briefings, safety protocols, and escort requests where appropriate.

travel_pages.meta_description: JVTO safety approach: Tourist Police-led planning, gear requirements, Ijen gas mask sterilisation, emergency protocols, and what guests must follow.
intro_paragraphs: JVTO tours are designed as private-only, pre-briefed programs with defined safety procedures — led by an active Tourist Police officer.
This page explains how safety is built into route design, decisions, and your responsibilities as a guest.
Official channels: Website https://javavolcano-touroperator.com • WhatsApp +6282244788833 • Email hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com.

sections.title: 1\) Our Approach to Safety
paragraphs: Expect safety to be built into route design, partners, and documented processes. JVTO is presented as a licensed Indonesian inbound operator led by an active Tourist Police officer, and this is stated to influence how tours are designed and run.

Expect private-only operations with dedicated vehicles and briefings. Every JVTO tour is private-only: no open-group buses, no mixing with strangers, and your group gets its own vehicle, schedule, and briefings.

sections.title: 2\) Official Information Sources We Monitor
paragraphs: Expect JVTO to use official sources for volcano and geohazard context. Before and during your tour, JVTO states it follows official sources, in particular MAGMA Indonesia (PVMBG) for volcanic and geohazard information, hazard maps, and recommendations.
Use VONA notices only if you understand technical formats. VONA is described as a specialised service within MAGMA for aviation ash notices, and an example technical endpoint is provided:
[https://magma.esdm.go.id/v1/vona?code=KRA](https://magma.esdm.go.id/v1/vona?code=KRA)
This is described as technical and not required for guests to interpret directly.
Expect local authority announcements to influence access. JVTO references local authorities and park management updates for closures, restricted zones, and safety conditions.
Expect itinerary changes to follow related Travel Guide rules. When risk or restrictions affect plans, changes follow /travel-guide/weather-and-closures and /travel-guide/booking-information.

sections.title: 3\) How Safety Is Built into Each Tour
paragraphs: Expect professional transport, licensed local guides, and structured briefings. JVTO describes private vehicles with professional drivers, licensed local guides around key sites, and structured briefings before major activities.
Expect Ijen to add screening and gas management steps. Ijen routes include screening by trained medical staff, gas masks, and guidance on when to stop or turn back if conditions change.

Expect large groups to use formal escort logic where applicable. For large groups, JVTO may coordinate official traffic police escort on specific segments under /travel-guide/police-escort-for-groups.

sections.title: 4\) Your Responsibilities as a Guest
paragraphs: Treat safety as shared and dependent on honest information and compliance. Guests are asked to be honest about health and mobility, follow medical staff guidance during Ijen screening, respect restrictions, stay with the group, and tell guides immediately if serious symptoms occur.

Use appropriate gear and follow instructions to prevent risk escalation. Guests are instructed to bring and use appropriate gear as described in /travel-guide/packing-and-fitness and to follow guide instructions and marked paths.

sections.title: 5\) Volcanic Alerts, Weather & Itinerary Changes
paragraphs: Expect volcano and mountain weather to trigger changes quickly. JVTO describes monitoring sources, adjusting times/viewpoints/routes, explaining changes in plain language, and applying closure and reroute rules from /travel-guide/weather-and-closures and /travel-guide/booking-information.

Use official sources if you want national context, but do not rely on raw codes for decision-making. Guests may consult official MAGMA information and the VONA feed if familiar, but JVTO states it handles interpretation and planning.

sections.title: 6\) Ijen Health Screening and Medical Limits
paragraphs: Expect screening to be standard for Ijen routes and to influence participation. Screening is included, performed by trained staff, and may result in advice not to hike if unsafe.

Treat “cleared” outcomes as risk reduction, not risk elimination. A cleared screening reduces risk but does not make the hike risk-free, and guides retain the right to stop or turn back if conditions become unsafe.

sections.title: 7\) Insurance, Documents & Emergency Support
paragraphs: Arrange travel insurance that covers hiking and disruption risks. Insurance is strongly recommended for trekking/hiking, interruptions due to natural events, and medical evacuation and treatment.
Carry basic documents and emergency information. Guests are advised to carry passport copy, insurance details, and important contacts.

Expect JVTO to maintain internal route and contact readiness. JVTO describes keeping emergency contacts and route plans known to the office and maintaining access to local health facilities, following local/national guidance for evacuations or restrictions.

sections.title: 8\) Where to Go Next in the Travel Guide
paragraphs: Use these pages together as the official reference set.

sections.title: Intro
paragraphs: Use this page to understand how closures and changing conditions can affect a private tour and how JVTO responds. Volcanoes and waterfalls are part of a changing natural environment, and this page explains how weather, volcanic activity, and access closures can affect your tour and how JVTO responds.

Use your E-Voucher and Booking Policy for binding details. This is a plain-language summary; for legal details and specific cases, your Official E-Voucher and the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy are the final reference.

sections.title: 1\) Factors That Can Change a Volcano or Waterfall Tour
paragraphs: Expect access and timing to change due to natural events and official regulations. Routes can be influenced by heavy rain, fog, strong winds, lightning, ash, gas emissions, landslides, floods, road damage, forest fires, smoke, ceremonies, and temporary rules.
Expect partial impacts or full closure depending on severity. Sometimes only specific areas are affected; sometimes full closure applies for safety and legal reasons.

sections.title: 2\) How JVTO Monitors Conditions & Decides on Changes
paragraphs: Expect decisions to use local reports and official inputs. JVTO uses local guide/driver reports, park and government information, and updates linked to alert levels, gas, and weather.

Expect changes when conditions are unsafe or access is restricted. When conditions are clearly unsafe or formally restricted, JVTO adjusts plans in line with regulations, safety considerations, and the options allowed under the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy.

sections.title: 3\) Partial Closures vs Full Closures
paragraphs: Expect JVTO to distinguish partial closures from full closures. Not all changes are the same, and restrictions may affect certain viewpoints, rim sections, crater floor access, or lower paths at waterfalls.

Expect partial closures to keep a modified structure where possible. In partial closures, JVTO typically adapts to what is still legally and safely accessible while keeping the main structure of your tour.

sections.title: 4\) Alternative Routes & Adjustments
paragraphs: Expect JVTO to propose alternatives where feasible and explain how they relate to policy. JVTO will inform you as early as reasonably possible, propose alternative viewpoints/routes/activities where available, and explain how changes relate to the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and your Official E-Voucher.
Expect some substitutions to differ from the original experience. Some alternatives may not fully match the original plan but are chosen to keep your trip running safely and legally.

sections.title: 5\) Blue Fire, Sunrise Views & Visibility
paragraphs: Do not treat blue fire or perfect sunrise views as guarantees. Blue fire and specific views depend on wind, gas, clouds, fog, crowd control, and regulations.
Treat photos as examples, not promises. Screenshots or photos on the website and social media are examples of past conditions, not promises for your specific date.

sections.title: 6\) Closures, Travel Credit & External Events
paragraphs: Expect closure responses to follow official decisions and your booking documents. JVTO aligns with authorities, applies options described in your Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and Official E-Voucher, and may continue with a modified route or apply Travel Credit where applicable.
Use this page for principles, not every scenario. This page explains guiding principles rather than every possible scenario.

sections.title: 7\) Your Role as a Guest During Uncertain Conditions
paragraphs: Help keep the tour safe by following briefings and accepting reasonable flexibility. Read briefings, follow instructions from crew and authorities, and allow flexibility when conditions change plans.

sections.title: 8\) Which Document Wins if There Is a Difference?
paragraphs: Follow the document hierarchy if wording differs. Priority order: Official E-Voucher/Invoice → Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy → Inclusions & Exclusions Policy → general website and informal communication.

travel_pages.meta_description: What to pack and how fit you need to be for Bromo, Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu tours. Specific gear list, fitness expectations, and what JVTO provides.
travel_pages.h1: Packing & Fitness for Bromo, Ijen & Tumpak Sewu
intro_paragraphs: Bromo, Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu are non-technical routes but physically demanding. This page covers what to pack and what fitness level to expect — by destination.
This is a preparation guide, not a medical evaluation. Final Ijen clearance comes from the included health screening.
Official channels: Website https://javavolcano-touroperator.com • WhatsApp +6282244788833 • Email hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com.

sections.title: 1\) What to Expect Physically on Each Tour
paragraphs: Expect these routes to be non-technical but physically demanding. Bromo, Ijen, and Tumpak Sewu are not technical climbs, but they can still involve long days, early departures, night-time sections (especially Ijen), altitude and temperature changes, uneven ground, stairs, and steep slopes.

Use this guide for preparation, not medical clearance. This page helps you prepare but is not a medical evaluation, and final Ijen clearance comes from the included health screening and the medical staff who perform it.

sections.title: 2\) Simple Fitness Checklist (Self-Assessment)
paragraphs: Use a basic self-check before booking. Ask yourself if you can walk 3–4 hours with breaks, handle early mornings or night starts, walk on uneven or dusty ground without losing balance easily, and manage steep wet paths and handholds at Tumpak Sewu.

Consult a doctor and inform JVTO early if you have serious concerns. If you are unsure or have a known heart, lung, or serious mobility issue, talk to your doctor first and tell JVTO in your booking form.
Expect Ijen screening to affect go/no-go decisions. For Ijen, fitness is additionally checked through screening, which may result in you being advised not to hike if it is unsafe.

sections.title: 3\) Essentials to Pack for All Volcano Tours
paragraphs: Pack footwear, layers, and a small daypack as baseline essentials. Useful items include closed walking shoes or light hiking boots with good grip, breathable layers, warm mid-layer, windproof/waterproof outer layer, trousers suitable for wet conditions, extra socks, and a daypack with rain cover.
Pack basic weather and sun items for changing conditions. Bring a hat or beanie, light gloves, sunglasses, sunscreen, and lip balm.
Keep personal items practical and secure. Bring essential medication, toiletries, a power bank, and a copy of your passport photo page (printed or on phone).
Expect main luggage to stay in vehicle/hotel during activities. You carry only what you need for each activity as advised in briefings.

sections.title: 4\) Extra Items for Mount Bromo
paragraphs: Pack extra warmth and dust comfort items for sunrise. Bring additional warm layers, beanie, gloves, and consider a buff/scarf/light mask for dust and smell comfort.
Expect cold mornings to affect batteries. Bring enough battery for camera/phone; cold and early wake-ups can drain batteries faster.

sections.title: 5\) Extra Items for Ijen Crater Tours
paragraphs: Use JVTO-provided gear but bring comfort items if helpful. JVTO provides gas masks, trekking poles when needed, mineral water, and depending on setup, a headlamp/flashlight.
Bring items that improve comfort under night conditions. Consider a scarf/buff, thin inner gloves, and light snacks you tolerate well.
Treat Ijen as a steep, sustained hike with variable gas exposure. Be honest about heart/lung/circulation issues and follow screening and current-condition decisions.

sections.title: 6\) Extra Items for Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
paragraphs: Prepare for wet, steep, and slippery terrain. Bring shoes with strong grip that can get wet or sports sandals with heel strap and traction, quick-drying clothes, a poncho/rain jacket, waterproof protection for valuables, and dry clothes for after the hike.
Avoid this route if serious mobility issues apply unless discussed. This route is not ideal for serious knee/hip/balance issues; inform JVTO in advance.

sections.title: 7\) Weather, Fitness & Safety Information
paragraphs: Use the related Travel Guide pages for closure, screening, and safety procedures. See:
Treat preparation as shared responsibility. Packing well and knowing your limits support safety and enjoyment, while JVTO plans, briefs, and adjusts based on conditions and official guidance.

paragraphs: Use this page to understand how JVTO private tour bookings work from deposit to Travel Credit. This page explains, in simple terms, how private tour bookings with Java Volcano Tour Operator work—from choosing a tour and paying the deposit, to final payments, changes, cancellations, and Travel Credit.

sections.title: 1\) Private Tours Only & Official Channels
paragraphs: Book JVTO tours as private, all-inclusive packages only. All JVTO routes are private, all-inclusive tours—we do not sell shared-group or transport-only packages.

Protect your booking by using only official JVTO channels and checkout. Book only through the official JVTO website and secure checkout, and use only the official WhatsApp number and email address listed on this site.
Verify anything unusual before paying. If you ever receive a different account number, payment link, or contact, pause and verify it with us via our official contacts before paying.

sections.title: 2\) How to Book a Private Tour with JVTO
paragraphs: Confirm a booking by paying through secure checkout and receiving an Official E-Voucher. Your booking is considered confirmed only when payment has been processed and your Official E-Voucher has been issued to your verified email address.
Step-by-step

Use official documents for precise wording. This page is a plain-language summary; for precise legal wording and conditions, refer to the Official Booking Guide – How to Book and the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy.
bullets: Choose your tour on the official website. Select your starting point (for example, Surabaya or Bali), your preferred duration (2D1N, 3D2N, 4D3N, 5D+), and review the detailed itinerary, inclusions, and exclusions.

Provide lead guest contact details used for delivery and support. Enter the full name of the lead guest, a verified email address, and an active WhatsApp/mobile number so we can issue your Official E-Voucher and support your booking before and during the tour.

Pay the initial deposit (or full amount if required). In most cases, a 20% deposit is required at checkout; if Day 1 is close (for example, less than 14 days away), the system may request up to 100% full payment at the time of booking.

Receive your payment confirmation and Official E-Voucher. After a successful payment, you will see a successful/complete status and receive an order summary/payment receipt and your Official E-Voucher (PDF). Always check the details and contact us quickly if something is incorrect.

Provide additional details after confirmation if requested. Once confirmed, you may receive a secure link or access to a My Bookings/Customer Portal to provide extra details such as guest list, arrival times, room preferences, dietary needs, and relevant health information.

sections.title: 3\) Payments – Deposit, Balance & Accepted Methods
paragraphs: 3.1 Deposit

Secure most bookings with a 20% deposit unless clearly stated otherwise. Standard rule: a 20% deposit of the total package price is required to secure a booking, unless a different amount is clearly stated in writing for special products or promotions.
Expect up to 100% payment for close departures. For tours where Day 1 is less than 14 days away, the system may request up to 100% full payment at checkout.
3.2 How you can pay
Pay the initial deposit by card through secure checkout. The initial deposit is paid by credit/debit card only, through JVTO’s secure, SSL-encrypted checkout with an authorised payment gateway.
Pay the remaining balance using the deadline that matches your method. For the remaining balance (if only 20% was paid initially):

Expect consequences if deadlines are missed. If balance payment deadlines are not met, your booking may be handled according to the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy, which can include cancellation and forfeiture depending on timing.
3.3 Payment security

Use secure checkout and do not share sensitive payment credentials. All online card payments are processed via a secure, encrypted checkout using a compliant payment gateway, and JVTO does not store full card numbers or CVV codes on its own systems.
Treat requests for card details or passwords as invalid. JVTO will never ask you to send full card details or online banking passwords via WhatsApp, email, or social media.
Stop and verify unusual instructions. If you receive unusual payment instructions, stop and verify using the official WhatsApp or email listed on this site.
bullets: Card (online): pay via JVTO’s secure payment link no later than 5 calendar days before Day 1\.
Bank transfer / Wise: pay to JVTO’s official company bank accounts no later than 3 calendar days before Day 1\.
Cash at JVTO office: possible only if explicitly approved in writing at the time of booking; must be settled in Indonesian Rupiah at the JVTO office before departure on Day 1\.

sections.title: 4\) Cancellations, Travel Credit & No-Show (Guest-Initiated)
paragraphs: 4.1 Cancel 48 hours or more before Day 1

Receive Travel Credit instead of a cash refund when cancelling early enough. If you cancel 48 hours or more before Day 1 (local Indonesia time), any payments already received are not refunded in cash.
Receive Travel Credit equal to 100% of eligible payments received. JVTO issues Travel Credit equal to 100% of the payments received for the cancelled services.
Use Travel Credit with these characteristics. Travel Credit:
4.2 Cancel less than 48 hours before Day 1
Expect forfeiture for late cancellations. If you cancel less than 48 hours before Day 1, up to 100% of the total package price may be forfeited.
Do not expect Travel Credit or cash refunds for very late cancellations. As a general rule, no Travel Credit or cash refund is provided when cancelling this late.
4.3 No-show & same-day cancellation

Treat no-shows and same-day cancellations as forfeiture cases. Situations such as not appearing at the pickup point, refusing to depart after vehicles and crew are deployed, cancelling on the same day after services are prepared, or voluntarily leaving after the tour starts may be treated as no-show/same-day cancellation.
Expect up to 100% forfeiture and no unused-service credit. Up to 100% of the total package price may be forfeited, and no refund or Travel Credit is provided for services not used.
4.4 Skipping activities or leaving early

Keep the full package payable if you skip activities for personal reasons. If a guest chooses to skip activities or end the tour early for personal reasons, no partial refund or discount applies and the full package price remains payable.
bullets: Transferable/giftable with written confirmation from JVTO
Applied to future JVTO private tours at prevailing rates at the time of the new booking
No rebooking or administration fee for using valid Travel Credit

sections.title: 5\) Changing Your Booking (Dates, Details & Adjustments)
paragraphs: Expect minor corrections to be possible and operational changes to carry supplier costs. Some minor corrections may be possible without extra charge, but changes that affect permits, tickets, accommodation allocations, or transport inventory typically pass supplier costs on to the guest.
Follow formal rules for reschedules. Reschedule options follow the timings and rules described in the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and on your Official E-Voucher.

sections.title: 6\) Changes by JVTO & External Events
paragraphs: Expect JVTO to adjust plans when required for safety, compliance, or access. JVTO may adjust routes, timings, accommodation, or activities due to volcanic activity, toxic gas, landslides, floods, government restrictions, or park closures.

Expect alternatives where feasible and policy-based remedies. JVTO prioritises safety and legal compliance, proposes alternatives where feasible, and applies options described in the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and your Official E-Voucher.
Use the policy for force majeure detail. Full details of how force majeure and external events are handled are described in the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy.

sections.title: 7\) Your Responsibilities & Document Hierarchy
paragraphs: Provide accurate information and verify your E-Voucher. Provide accurate names (as per ID/passport where required), contact details, arrival/departure times, and relevant health information you choose to share, and review your Official E-Voucher carefully.
Use only official channels and payment methods. Use only official channels and verified payment instructions.

travel_pages.title_tag: Traffic Police Escort for Tourist Groups in East Java | JVTO
travel_pages.meta_description: How official traffic police escorts work for larger tourist groups in East Java. When it is appropriate, how JVTO coordinates it, and what it involves.
travel_pages.h1: Traffic Police Escort for Tourist Groups in East Java
intro_paragraphs: For larger tourist groups, JVTO can coordinate official traffic police escort on specific road segments in East Java. This page explains what that means, when it applies, and how it is requested.
Escort is never guaranteed, never informal, and never automatic. Details must be confirmed in writing in your booking documents.
Official channels: Website https://javavolcano-touroperator.com • WhatsApp +6282244788833 • Email hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com.

sections.title: Intro
paragraphs: Use this page to understand what official traffic police escort is, when it is appropriate, and how it is requested. This page explains how official traffic police escort can be coordinated for certain larger groups travelling with JVTO in East Java.
Do not treat escort as guaranteed or informal. Escort is not guaranteed for every group and is not an offer of unofficial or informal privileges.

sections.title: 1\) What “Traffic Police Escort” Means in Practice
paragraphs: Treat escort as a legally authorised convoy support service. Escort refers to official traffic police support such as marked police vehicles guiding a convoy on specific segments and assisting convoy movement through junctions.
Treat escort as safety support—not rule bypass. Escort is not a license to ignore traffic laws, a guarantee of higher speeds, or an informal favour sold as a trick.

sections.title: 2\) When Can a Traffic Police Escort Be Considered?
paragraphs: Consider escort only for larger, multi-vehicle convoys on specific segments. JVTO generally considers requesting escort when the group is significantly larger (for example, around 18 guests or more), multiple vehicles move together, and certain segments benefit from coordinated movement.
Treat approval as conditional and authority-driven. Escort is never automatic and depends on law, regulations, availability, and police priorities.

sections.title: 3\) How Requests Are Made & Approved
paragraphs: Expect a documented request and written approval process. JVTO can discuss escort during planning, prepare route/timing details, and submit a formal request through relevant channels.
Expect tours to proceed without escort if not approved. If not approved, the tour runs without escort and JVTO applies its convoy and safety procedures within normal traffic rules.

sections.title: 4\) Costs & What Is Included (If Approved)
paragraphs: Expect additional costs to be agreed in advance and documented. Costs may include escort-related charges and operational adjustments, and any costs are discussed with the organiser, stated in writing, and included in the Official E-Voucher/Invoice if confirmed.
Do not expect unofficial roadside payments. There is no “cash-on-the-road” payment for unofficial escort services offered by JVTO; charges are documented as part of booking where applicable.

sections.title: 5\) What an Escort Does – And What It Is Not
paragraphs: Use escort to support orderly convoy movement only. Escort is meant to support safe movement and coordination on approved segments.
Do not use escort as a speed promise or rule override. Escort is not intended to break regulations or guarantee travel times, and standard safety expectations still apply.

sections.title: 6\) Behaviour Expectations for Groups with Escort
paragraphs: Follow instructions and keep convoy behaviour orderly. Guests must follow instructions from JVTO staff and traffic police, drivers remain responsible for safe driving, and distracting or inappropriate behaviour is not acceptable.

sections.title: 7\) How JVTO’s Tourist Police Experience Fits Into This
paragraphs: Use JVTO’s experience to support legal, documented coordination only. The founder’s professional experience is described as helping JVTO understand legal escort requests and communicate with relevant units.
Do not treat this as a promise of escort availability. JVTO does not promise escort will always be available and does not offer unofficial escort services.

sections.title: 8\) Where to Check Your Group’s Exact Arrangement
paragraphs: Treat escort as included only if written in your booking documents. Final details appear in your Official E-Voucher/Invoice and any written JVTO confirmation; if escort is not mentioned, assume the programme runs without escort.

travel_pages.title_tag: Ijen Health Screening – Real Checks, Digital Proof & QR | JVTO
travel_pages.meta_description: How Ijen health screening works with JVTO: real checks by trained staff, digital records with QR codes, and a public verification link for every guest.
travel_pages.h1: Ijen Health Screening – Real Checks, Digital Proof
intro_paragraphs: Kawah Ijen is a night hike at altitude with possible sulfur gas exposure. JVTO integrates mandatory health screening before the crater hike — not as a formality, but as a practical safety step.
Screening is included in your JVTO Ijen package. Results are digitally recorded and verifiable via QR code.
Official channels: Website https://javavolcano-touroperator.com • WhatsApp +6282244788833 • Email hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com.

sections.title: 1\) Why Ijen Health Screening Matters
paragraphs: Ijen screening exists because Kawah Ijen is demanding and exposure conditions can be risky. Kawah Ijen is a night hike with steep sections, cold temperatures, and possible sulfur gas exposure.

Ijen screening also exists to reduce fake or incomplete checks. In the past, some visitors reached the gate without real screening or with fake letters, putting visitors, local teams, and authorities at risk.
JVTO supports a system where screening is real and verifiable. Guests attempt the hike only after a real screening, and results can be checked digitally to prove screening occurred.

sections.title: 2\) For JVTO Guests – What We Include
paragraphs: Ijen night-hike tours include screening as part of your JVTO package. If your tour includes the Ijen night hike, your package includes a health screening before the hike.

Expect screening to follow a practical, documented workflow. Screening typically happens before departure for the night hike at a partner accommodation or clinic, performed by trained medical staff, and includes basic measurements and relevant questions.
Expect digital recording and QR linkage. The result is recorded in a secure digital system and linked to a QR code.
Use your E-Voucher and briefing for exact timing and requirements. Your Official E-Voucher and daily briefing will show where/when screening occurs and what you need to prepare.

sections.title: 3\) For Non-JVTO Travellers – Using the Public Digital Tool
paragraphs: Non-JVTO travellers can use the same workflow through a public tool. The digital screening workflow is described as available for other travellers through a public web tool.

Follow the register → screen → QR clearance flow. You register basic details and intended hiking time, attend screening with a participating provider, and if cleared, receive a QR-linked clearance that gate staff can verify.
Use the official access instructions for the tool. For access to the public tool, follow information on health.mountijen.com or as communicated by local authorities and official providers.

sections.title: 4\) At the Ijen Gate – How the QR Is Used
paragraphs: Expect gate staff to verify that screening occurred. Staff may ask if you completed screening and may check clearance via QR code or reference.

Treat QR verification as safety traceability, not bureaucracy. The purpose is to ensure screening is real and traceable, reduce falsified letters, and help identify guests who may not be fit for a demanding hike.

sections.title: 5\) If the Screening Says “Not Recommended to Hike”
paragraphs: Do not hike if screening suggests it is unsafe. JVTO will not force the hike and will respect screening results.
Expect itinerary alternatives to follow policy and your E-Voucher. JVTO will discuss alternatives in line with the Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy and your Official E-Voucher.
Do not assume screening outcomes automatically produce refunds. Health-related decisions are serious and may affect what is possible, but they do not automatically mean a full refund.

sections.title: 6\) What This Screening Is – And What It Is Not
paragraphs: Use screening as a practical risk filter and verification step. It helps identify basic risks before a demanding night hike and provides a verifiable record that screening took place.
Do not treat screening as a guarantee or full medical evaluation. It is not a hospital admission, full medical check-up, or guarantee of safety, and conditions can still change quickly.

sections.title: 7\) Data & Privacy
paragraphs: Treat screening data as limited to what is required for clearance. The system records only the information required to operate the screening and clearance process.
Use the dedicated Privacy Policy for details when finalised. Details on collection, retention, and protection are described as belonging in a dedicated Privacy Policy once finalised.

sections.title: 8\) Quick Questions About Ijen Screening
paragraphs: Q1. Is Ijen health screening mandatory? Screening is stated as standard for JVTO guests attempting the Ijen night hike, and non-JVTO visitors are strongly encouraged to use official screening channels; local authorities may require proof at certain times.
Q2. Does screening guarantee that nothing will happen? No; screening reduces risk but does not guarantee outcomes, and access may still change due to conditions.
Q3. Does screening affect my insurance? Screening is a safety/verification step and not a replacement for travel insurance; guests remain responsible for arranging appropriate coverage.

sections.title: What makes Ijen different
paragraphs: Kawah Ijen contains the world's largest acidic crater lake (pH ~0.5) and is one of only two locations globally where the blue fire phenomenon is reliably visible — caused by ignited sulfuric gas emerging at 600°C.

Sulfur miners carry loads of 60–100 kg from the crater floor twice daily. Guides provide context without making the hike a spectacle.

policy_pages.body_md:
# JVTO Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy

Policy Route: `/policy/booking-payment-cancellation/`

### 1) Operator Identity & Official Channels

This policy applies to all enquiries, quotations, bookings, amendments, cancellations, and tour participation with Java Volcano Tour Operator (JVTO), operated by **PT Java Volcano Rendezvous**.

**Official channels (valid booking, support, and payment verification):**

- Website & secure checkout: `https://javavolcano-touroperator.com`
- WhatsApp: `+62 822-4478-8833`
- Email: `hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com`

JVTO is not responsible for any offers, instructions, or payment requests made outside these official channels.

---

### 2) Order of Precedence (If There Is Any Discrepancy)

If there is any discrepancy, the following order applies:

1. The specific terms on your **Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)** for the confirmed booking
2. This **Booking, Payment & Cancellation Policy**
3. The **Inclusions & Exclusions Policy**
4. The **Privacy & Data Protection Policy**
5. The **Travel Guide — Booking Information** (/travel-guide/booking-information)

---

### 3) Nature of JVTO Products

- JVTO operates **private, all-inclusive tours**.
- JVTO does not operate open join-in group tours and does not sell standalone “transport-only” services as its core product.
- Only inclusions explicitly stated on the official tour page and/or your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) are contractually binding.

---

### 4) When a Booking Is Confirmed

A booking is confirmed **only** when all of the following are met:

1. You select a specific official JVTO package; and
2. Your required payment (deposit or full payment) is successfully processed via approved methods; and
3. JVTO issues your **Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)** confirming the booking details.

Enquiries, messages, and quotations are not binding until the booking is confirmed under the rule above.

---

### 5) Pricing & Currency

- Official pricing and accounting are in **Indonesian Rupiah (IDR)**.

---

### 6) Payments

#### 6.1 Deposit Requirement

- Standard deposit: **20%** of the total booking value.
- If Day 1 is within **14 days**, JVTO may require up to **100%** full payment.

#### 6.2 Approved Payment Methods

**Deposit (initial payment):**

- Card payment via JVTO secure checkout (credit/debit).

**Balance payment (if applicable):**

- Card payment via secure checkout (deadline below), or
- Bank transfer / Wise (deadline below), or
- Cash at JVTO office (only if approved in writing).

#### 6.3 Balance Payment Deadlines (If Applicable)

- By card: no later than **5 days before Day 1**
- By bank transfer / Wise: no later than **3 days before Day 1**

#### 6.4 Bank Transfer Details (Public)

Use these details only for **balance payments** when instructed through official channels.

- Bank BRI — PT Java Volcano Rendezvous — 001301001779564 — SWIFT: BRINIDJAXXX
- Bank BCA — PT Java Volcano Rendezvous — 1200944352 — SWIFT: CENAIDJAXXX

Any payment instructions outside the above must be verified via official channels before action.

#### 6.5 Payment Security & Anti-Fraud

- Checkout is SSL-encrypted and processed via a PCI DSS-compliant payment gateway.
- JVTO does not store full card numbers, CVV codes, or online banking passwords.
- JVTO will never ask you for full card details, CVV, or banking credentials via chat or email.  
  If you are unsure, verify via WhatsApp or email before paying.

---

### 7) Amendments & Rescheduling

#### 7.1 Amendments

If you need to correct booking details (e.g., passenger name spelling, pickup details, rooming requests), notify JVTO as soon as possible. Some changes may be subject to supplier rules, availability, and cost differences.

#### 7.2 Rescheduling (Date Change)

- Requests made **48 hours or more before Day 1**: one reschedule for the same package may be granted without fee, subject to availability and any seasonal/cost differences.
- Requests made **less than 48 hours before Day 1**: generally not permitted and may be treated as a late cancellation.

---

### 8) Cancellation Policy (48-Hour Cut-Off)

#### Definitions

- **Day 1**: the first day of services for your tour.
- Cut-off time is measured in **local Indonesia time**.

#### 8.1 Guest-Initiated Cancellation (48 Hours or More Before Day 1)

- Payments are **not refundable in cash**.
- **100% converts to JVTO Travel Credit**.

**Travel Credit rules:**

- Issued in **IDR**
- **Non-expiring**
- **Transferable** with written confirmation
- Can be used for future tours at prevailing rates

#### 8.2 Guest-Initiated Cancellation (Less Than 48 Hours Before Day 1)

- The booking may be forfeited up to **100%**.
- Travel Credit and cash refunds are generally **not provided**.

---

### 9) Force Majeure & External Events

JVTO may partially or fully cancel, postpone, or reroute a tour due to events beyond reasonable control, including (but not limited to):

- Volcanic activity, toxic gas, earthquakes, landslides, floods
- Government or park closures or restrictions
- Strikes, civil unrest, security threats
- Major infrastructure or transportation disruptions
- Severe weather or conditions rendering services unsafe

#### Compensation Principles (JVTO-Initiated)

Where JVTO cancels or significantly alters services not due to guest breach, non-payment, or no-show, JVTO may offer one or more of the following (considering irrecoverable third-party costs):

- Reasonable alternative arrangements of comparable value
- JVTO Travel Credit covering the unused portion
- A partial or full cash refund, net of non-recoverable expenses, as agreed in writing

Guest-initiated cancellations remain governed by Section 8.

---

### 10) Health, Fitness & Eligibility

- Guests are responsible for ensuring they are medically and physically fit for the itinerary.
- Certain activities (e.g., volcano hikes, night trekking, sulfur gas exposure) may be unsuitable for specific conditions.
- Where mandatory health screening or permit conditions are imposed by authorities (e.g., Mount Ijen):

  - Guests must comply with the process arranged or recognised by JVTO
  - If a guest is denied clearance, affected components are **non-refundable**, as related costs are pre-committed

- JVTO strongly recommends comprehensive travel insurance including medical cover and trip interruption.

---

### 11) Guest Responsibilities

By confirming a booking with JVTO, the lead guest and all participants agree to:

- Provide accurate and complete information during enquiry and booking
- Make payments through official channels and within deadlines
- Review the Official E-Voucher promptly and report discrepancies
- Submit pickup/logistical details within requested timeframes
- Be present on time at the agreed pickup point
- Follow safety instructions from JVTO crew and licensed guides
- Respect local laws, cultures, communities, and environments
- Hold appropriate personal travel insurance

Failure to meet these responsibilities may limit JVTO’s ability to deliver services and may trigger consequences set out in this policy.

---

### 12) Group Incentives (FOC) — JVTO Website Only

We do offer FOC (Free of Charge) incentives based on group size.
Our current FOC scheme is as follows:

- 18 paying pax → 1 FOC
- 35 paying pax → 2 FOC
- 50 paying pax → 3 FOC

“Pax” refers to **paying guests only**.

---

policy_pages.body_md:
# JVTO Inclusions & Exclusions Policy

Policy Route: `/policy/inclusions-exclusions/`

### 1) Purpose

This policy defines what is included and not included in JVTO private tour packages. Only inclusions written on your specific **Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)** are contractually binding.

---

### 2) Contractual Rule (Write-it-to-Bind-it)

In this policy, “Official Tour Voucher” refers to your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) issued by JVTO after successful payment.

Only inclusions that appear on:

- the official JVTO package page for your chosen tour; and/or
- your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)

are legally binding components of your package.

If it is not written in your confirmed inclusions and voucher, it is not part of the contracted package, even if discussed informally.

---

### 3) Standard Inclusions (All Eligible JVTO Packages)

Unless stated otherwise on your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF), a confirmed JVTO private tour package typically includes:

#### 3.1 Transportation

- Private air-conditioned vehicle for all land sectors listed in your confirmed itinerary
- Fuel, tolls, and standard parking fees as per itinerary
- Vehicle allocation by group size (comfort and safety):

  - 2–3 guests: 1 × MPV (e.g., Toyota Avanza or similar)
  - 4–9 guests: 1 × Toyota Hiace (or similar 16-seat minibus)
  - 10–11 guests: 1 × Toyota Hiace + 1 × MPV (for seating and luggage space)

- 4WD Jeep at Mount Bromo (when Bromo is included):

  - Private jeep(s) for sunrise and crater viewpoints
  - Max. ±4 guests per jeep for comfort and safety

#### 3.2 Accommodation

- Hotel / homestay accommodation for all overnight stays listed in your itinerary
- Standard allocation (unless otherwise stated on your voucher):

  - Even-numbered groups: 1 room per 2 guests (King or Twin)
  - Odd-numbered groups: 1 room per 2 guests + 1 extra bed in one room

- Rooming principles:

  - Maximum 2 guests per standard room (excluding the designated extra bed where applicable)
  - King beds for couples or guests preferring more space
  - Twin beds for guests sharing a room

- Upgrade option (subject to request and availability): replacing an extra bed with an additional private room may be available at a published supplement (as per voucher)

#### 3.3 Professional Crew

- Licensed, vetted crew adjusted by group size and route:

  - 2–3 guests: 1 × driver-guide (English-speaking) + licensed local site guides where required
  - 4–11 guests: 1 × professional driver + 1 × escort guide + licensed local site guides at key locations
  - Additional drivers allocated when extra vehicles are used

- Crew duties include: safe driving, time management, coordination with hotels/jeeps/ferries/park authorities, and basic route explanations and guest support

#### 3.4 Tickets, Permits & Local Logistics

All listed entrance tickets and environmental/park permits connected to your confirmed itinerary, where applicable, such as:

- Mount Bromo National Park access
- Mount Ijen (including regular entrance and applicable summit/crater access where legally permitted)
- Madakaripura Waterfall
- Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
- Other named attractions in your confirmed program
- Local retribution / standard local fees where explicitly built into the package

#### 3.5 Mount Ijen Safety & Trekking Support (When Ijen is Included)

- Professional gas mask (rated for sulfur environment) for each participating guest
- Trekking poles (shared or individual, depending on configuration)
- Mandatory Ijen medical check & digital/printed clearance arranged by JVTO, conducted by licensed medical personnel at an approved facility or at your hotel in the Bondowoso area (included in the package for itineraries that visit Ijen)

#### 3.6 Meals & Water

- Daily bottled mineral water in the vehicle for overland sectors
- Hotel breakfasts for each included overnight stay
- Any additional included meals (e.g., set dinners or lunches) are included only when explicitly stated on the official package page and/or your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)

#### 3.7 Java-Bali Ferry (Where Applicable)

Ferry tickets for Java-Bali or Bali-Java sectors when:

- the crossing is listed in your itinerary; and
- the ticket is specified as included on your voucher

#### 3.8 JVTO Branded Inclusions & Group Extras

- 1 × JVTO travel T-shirt per participant for eligible packages (standard JVTO design; size selection via “Complete Your Details”)
- Custom JVTO Group T-shirt (where explicitly included): for groups of ≥12 guests in a single booking, if written on your package/voucher; 1 × custom design T-shirt per person; sizes and final design must be confirmed before production

#### 3.9 On-Trip Support & Coordination

- Pre-trip assistance via official WhatsApp/email
- Clear pickup instructions and emergency contact numbers on your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)
- On-trip coordination for timing, weather/closure adjustments, and safety decisions
- Support in case of incident (basic first response, coordination with local clinics/hospitals, and logistics adjustments where feasible)

---

### 4) “We Offer More” — Conditional Inclusions (Only If Written)

The following are included **only** when clearly stated on both:

- the official package description you booked; and
- your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF)

Conditional inclusions may include:

- Custom JVTO group T-shirts (design, quantity, eligibility conditions)
- Additional hosted meals (e.g., dinner in Bondowoso before Ijen; lunch after Tumpak Sewu; special farewell dinner)
- Bali hotel/airport transfers beyond the standard ferry crossing
- Early check-in / late check-out at hotels
- Dedicated photographer/videographer services
- Police escort or special convoy arrangements for VIP/large groups (subject to availability and regulation)

**Extra meals — specific itinerary conditions (only if written):**

- For Ijen + Bondowoso overnight itineraries:

  - Dinner at the Bondowoso hotel before the Ijen night trek
  - Lunch at the Bondowoso hotel after returning from Ijen

- For itineraries including Tumpak Sewu:

  - One additional lunch at/near Yanto Homestay (or equivalent local partner) after the Tumpak Sewu program

---

### 5) Exclusions (Simple Principle)

To keep things clear: anything that is not explicitly listed as an inclusion on the official package page and on your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) is excluded.

Common examples of not included items (unless clearly written as included for your specific booking):

- International / domestic flights and entry visas
- Travel insurance of any kind
- Meals not specified (including alcohol)
- Tips/gratuities for guides and drivers

---

### 6) Discrepancies

This policy is designed to integrate with your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF). In the event of any discrepancy, the version referenced on your booking confirmation and voucher will apply.

policy_pages.body_md:
# JVTO Privacy & Data Protection Policy

Policy Route: `/policy/privacy`

### 1) Purpose

This policy explains what personal data JVTO collects, why it is collected, how it is used, and when it may be shared to operate your tour booking and comply with applicable requirements.

---

### 2) What Data We Collect

JVTO may collect the following categories of data, depending on your booking and itinerary requirements:

**2.1 Contact and booking details (lead guest):**

- Full name
- Email address
- WhatsApp/mobile number
- Booking references and tour details needed to deliver services

**2.2 Participant details (where required):**

- Guest list for accommodation and permits
- Passport/ID details and date of birth where required for permits, tickets, or legal compliance
- Pickup/drop-off details, rooming preferences, and operational notes required to deliver services

**2.3 Safety and logistics information (where relevant):**

- Dietary needs and relevant health information provided by you for safety planning and operational coordination (only where necessary)

---

### 3) Why We Collect and Use Your Data

JVTO uses personal data to:

- Issue and deliver your Official E-Voucher / Invoice (PDF) and manage your booking
- Coordinate logistics before and during your tour (pickup timing, accommodation, vehicles, guides, route planning)
- Provide customer support and operational assistance
- Meet legal, permit, ticketing, and accommodation registration requirements where applicable
- Support safety processes and emergency response coordination where needed

---

### 4) When We Share Your Data

JVTO shares personal data only when required to deliver the booked services or comply with applicable requirements. This may include sharing necessary details with:

- Accommodation partners (for check-in/registration and rooming)
- Drivers, escort guides, local site guides, and operational teams (for coordination and guest support)
- Ticketing/permit partners and local authorities where permits or entry requirements apply
- Medical screening providers or approved facilities where mandatory screening applies (e.g., where required by authorities for specific sites)

JVTO does not share data beyond what is necessary for the specific operational purpose.

---

### 5) Payment Data

- Card payments are processed through a secure checkout and authorised payment gateway.
- JVTO does not store full card numbers, CVV codes, or online banking passwords.

---

### 6) Data Protection and Access

JVTO applies internal controls to protect personal data and limits access to authorised personnel and partners only when needed for service delivery.

---

### 7) Contact

For privacy or data-related requests, contact:

- Email: `hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com`
- WhatsApp: `+62 822-4478-8833`

days.title: From Two Peaks: Ijen Adventure Continues to Mount Bromo
days.summary: After conquering Ijen, your adventure continues west. Enjoy our prepared lunch to refuel before embarking on the long overland journey to the majestic volcanic landscape of Bromo.

days.title: Two Icons in One Day: Bromo & Madakaripura, Back to Surabaya
days.summary: A day that combines the majesty of Bromo with the splendor of Madakaripura. After enjoying the sunrise, the adventure continues with a refreshing trek through the canyon of the legendary Madakaripura Waterfall, before we return you to Surabaya.

sections.title: What you need to bring and know
bullets: Sturdy hiking shoes and water shoes

sections.title: What is included
bullets: Private transport: AC vehicle (MPV 1–3 guests, Minibus 4–11), driver, fuel, tolls, parking
Tour guides: English-speaking. Driver-guide for 1–3 pax; separate local guide at each destination for 4+
Complimentary JVTO travel T-shirt (1 per participant)
Full assistance: arrival to final drop-off
Hotel accommodation: quality hotels, daily breakfast included
Private 4WD Jeep for Bromo sunrise tour
Ijen health screening: doctor check evening before trek (passport only required)
Trekking equipment: gas masks + trekking poles (Ijen); helmets at Madakaripura by local management
Ferry tickets: Ketapang Harbour → Gilimanuk Harbour (Bali)

sections.title: What is not included
bullets: Meals not stated in itinerary (own expense)
Personal expenses: snacks, souvenirs, beverages (carry min IDR 500,000/person)
Tips for drivers and guides (at discretion)

days.title: From the Blue Fire of Ijen to the Golden Sunset of Papuma
days.summary: Today was a mix of extreme adventure and relaxation. We began with a climb to Ijen, followed by lunch to recharge. Afterward, we headed south to the coast to close the day with a sunset at Papuma Beach.

days.title: Onward Transfer: From Jember to Tumpak Sewu Area
days.summary: After breakfast at your Jember hotel and perhaps some free time, the journey continues in the afternoon. This route connects downtown Jember with the rural areas of Lumajang, setting you up in a prime location for your Tumpak Sewu Waterfall adventure the next day.

days.title: Mount Bromo and Return to Surabaya's Civilization
days.summary: The day begins with a magical sunrise over Mount Bromo. After a volcanic adventure and a hearty breakfast, we'll drive you back down the mountains to Surabaya, concluding your adventure.
